methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gravitics is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Colin Doughan that has raised $20M plus $60M in STRATFI funding. The sector includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers ranging from SpaceX and Blue Origin to Sierra Space and Planet Labs, though the tightly-defined peer set at seed stage is sparse.

Stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation anchor. Using the $20M base funding raised as a proxy for seed valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $50M. This methodology is applicable given Gravitics' stage positioning, though reliance on a single method introduces concentration risk. Peer-median analysis cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort—while 25 same-sector peers exist broadly, granular filtering yields fewer than 3 datapoints. Public-comp anchoring using the 6-ticker Space & Aerospace median P/S of 42.9x is not feasible without the target's revenue, which private companies typically do not disclose.

The triangulation supports a $42M–$57M range, with a median estimate of $50M. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer benchmarking at seed stage, the reliance on stage-progression conventions rather than market-derived multiples, and opacity around the company's revenue generation and asset base relative to public comparables.
